channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Invincible VS Complete Character Tier List—Best Invincible VS Characters, Ranked
item.content:encoded:
The world of Invincible is full of superpowered people constantly beating each other up, so what better genre to suit the franchise than a tag fighter? Invincible VS is just that, and there are more than enough in-universe characters for the title to pull from, with 18 being selected at launch.

Just like the show and comics, though, some of the heroes (and villains) are stronger than the rest, with a select few of the roster certainly feeling a level above everyone else. In a fighting game where you’re always looking for an edge over your opponent, you’ll want to use the best the game has to offer.

That’s where this tier list should help you out. All 18 characters from Invincible VS have been ranked from worst to best, and you can find every entry below.

Invincible VS Tier List, Ranked

There are 18 base roster characters in Invincible VS, but only three of them make it into our S-tier ranking.

Now, before we dive into the rankings, it’s important to clarify that the game is still incredibly fresh. Metas are still being developed, and characters are still being torn apart to figure out how to best utilise their move sets.

  • More Insider Gaming: Invincible VS Story Mode Game Length & Difficulties

With that in mind, this tier list is prone to serious evolutions, and nobody’s place is set in stone. Get involved in the comments below and let us know if you think anybody needs to be moved, but until then, here is the complete Invincible VS character tier list, with S representing the best in class, and D showcasing the worst of the bunch.

Generally speaking, the characters in Invincible VS are very well balanced, but for my money, Omni-Man is the best meta-defining character in Invincible VS you need to be using right now.

All three characters in S-tier are exceptional, though they do offer slightly different playstyles. Dupli-Kate is incredibly quick, and you can pull off some insane combos with her kit. By contrast, Battle Beast is slower, but he packs a serious punch; time his moves well and you can devastate opponents.

  • More Insider Gaming: Does Invincible VS Have Crossplay? Cross-Platform Status, Explained

And then there’s Omni-Man, who feels like a perfect middle ground between the two. He can dish out some major damage, but he’s still fairly quick and easy to move around. Expect to see him in a lot of teams, especially partnered with either Battle Beast or Dupli-Kate.

All Invincible VS Characters Ranked From Worst To Best

As mentioned before, the game feels very balanced at launch, and I truly think every character can be a menace when in the right hands. Robot might rank lowest on the rankings (you’ll find out why soon), but I’ve played against people who make him seem like an S-tier pick.

All of this is to say that if you’re new to the game, give every character a try! You’ll undoubtedly have your own play style, and some characters will be better suited to it than others.

That being said, it’s time to go over our rankings, starting with 18th place.

18. Robot

Robot’s move set just never clicked with me. He always feels too far away from his opponent, and his movement is too stilted for my liking. He’s the only Balanced / Balanced character in the game, and he feels like that in gameplay—not really a master of anything.

17. Powerplex

In all honesty, Powerplex might be too low on this list, but nothing about his move set wowed me. He needs to be quite close to his opponent for some moves, whilst he does boast some decent range when you need to make some distance. He’s fine, but nothing to write home about.

16. Titan

Titan is best at close range, but his slow movement can make it frustrating to close gaps. When he is attacking, it’s slow and heavy, but that playstyle has never appealed to me too much, especially when other characters on the list can combo him fairly easily.

15. Cecil

Cecil is the master of ranged attacks, and fighting a good one can be hellish. He has numerous moves to keep you at range, but even when you get too close, he can quickly make some distance. I haven’t been able to utilize him to his fullest yet, but for some of you out there, he’s bound to be the perfect match.

14. Monster Girl

From here on out, I think every character is solid, and that begins with Monster Girl. She’s slow-moving, but her attacks can be quite the opposite, and you can put a heavy-hitting combo together fairly quickly. She might be too slow for some of you out there, but she makes a solid tank pick for your tag team.

13. Bulletproof

Bulletproof was a beast in the Invincible VS beta, but I think he’s been toned down slightly. He’s a fairly standard fighter, with some quick attacks and solid air-juggling capacities. I do find him quite basic, but if you’re new to the game, he’s a great pick to help you get acquainted with the gameplay.

12. Ella Mental

Fans of the show and comics might not recognise Ella Mental, and that’s because she’s exclusive to Invincible VS—and she’s a great addition! If it wasn’t obvious, she controls the elements and does her best work at a range, with fireballs and water missiles on offer.

That being said, she can do some work at close range, and she will definitely have a high skill ceiling for some players. I think she’s very well-rounded and could definitely climb up the rankings as time passes.

11. Rex Splode

Rex honestly feels really good to play. He’s quick, he has a great mix of ranged attacks, and he can move around the map very easily. For many, he could be higher on the list, and I wouldn’t have many complaints, but I found that when he winds up in the air, he can be a bit too prone to a counter.

10. Anissa

It took me a while to enjoy playing as Anissa, but the more I did, the more I enjoyed her move set. She is a bit basic, but her speed and ability to close gaps quickly really bump her up the ranks for me. I could see others having her lower-rated, but I think she has a very high skill ceiling with her Special Attacks—her dodge is insanely satisfying to pull off.

9. Invincible

The star of the show himself is a blast to play, and he’s the poster boy most will show up for. With that in mind, his character is nothing exceptional, but what he does, he does well, and that’s close down opponents quickly and hit reliable combos.

8. Allen The Alien

Allen was one of the top picks during the Beta, and he remains an excellent choice in the official launch. As large as he may be, he’s excellent at taking space and keeping the pressure on enemies, with some solid grappling and air combos on the cards, too.

7. Lucan

For those of you who have played the Story Mode, you’ll know that Lucan is incredibly strong and doesn’t let up at all. For better or for worse, he’s just as powerful in online play, and it’s due to his mix of grapple attacks and quick hits. Don’t let his gut fool you; this man is not to be messed with.

6. Thula

Thula is a great attacker who balances a lot of aspects well. Her Light attack is close range, but you can use your Medium to pull them in closer, before sending them into the air to hit a combo in the sky. In the right hands, she can trap you in a combo before you know it, and I can see her being fairly underrated.

5. Atom Eve

Eve has a very unique play style that I think a lot of people could love or hate. She’s not too great up close, but perfect at keeping an enemy right where you want them. Partner that with her solid ranged attacks and potential to air juggle with her hover, and she’s arguably the best zoner in the game.

4. Conquest

If you’ve watched Invincible, you’ll be glad to see that Conquest fights in-game just as well as he does on the show. He’s quick, he’s brutal, he’s incredibly powerful, and overall, a great pick. He could probably break into S-tier, but I do think that what he offers, both Battle Beast and Omni-Man, do just as well, if not slightly better.

3. Dupli-Kate

Dupli-Kate is one of my favorite characters to play in Invincible VS. I love her speed, and the combo potential with her is through the roof. She’s great fun to keep the pressure on opponents, and I can see a lot of people picking Bulletproof over her, but her variety tips the scales for me.

2. Battle Beast

There’s not much that differentiates first and second, but Battle Beast is undeniably one of the best fighters in the game. He hits hard, closes distances quickly, and can put together some mean combos, even in the air. Most opposing teams will have him in the squad, so if you won’t pick him, at least learn to face off against him.

1. Omni-Man

It should come as no surprise that Omni-Man is arguably the best character in Invincible VS. As his description suggests, he packs raw power, and does everything else just as well. He’s well-balanced, easy to use, and once mastered, a formidable opponent.

And there it is, the Invincible VS tier list. What do you make of the rankings? There are definitely some rogue shouts in there, so drop your thoughts in the comments or in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Invincible VS content, check out the following:

  • All Invincible VS Codes & Twitch Drops—How to Redeem Them, Explained

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

item.title: Forza Horizon 6 Multiplayer Modes Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Playground Games has revealed all the new and old multiplayer game modes players can try out in Forza Horizon 6.

Forza Horizon 6 is all set to release next month, and interested readers can check out details on the pre-order bonus and game editions here. Ahead of the launch, the developers have been sharing small tidbits about the upcoming title, and the latest focuses on FH6’s multiplayer modes.

Forza Horizon 6 Multiplayer Modes Feature Spec Racing, Togue Showdown, and More

The latest X post (formerly Twitter) from the official Forza Horizon 6 account offers players a brief glimpse of the upcoming game modes. The title will feature old game modes such as The Eliminator and Hide & Seek, along with new modes such as Spec Racing and Togue Showdown. Learn more about all the FH6 multiplayer modes that Playground Games has revealed below:

  • Horizon Racing: Featuring races across road, dirt, cross country, and street races,
  • Spec Racing: Features races on the road and dirt, but everybody has to be in the same car
  • Togue Showdown: Togue Racing is a Japan-inspired 1v1 mountain pass race, where players will go head-to-head in Togue championships.
  • Horizon Drift: It’s a drifting championship, where the highest score wins.
  • The Eliminator: A battle royale-style mode where players have to be the last driver standing.
  • Hide & Seek: This is a game mode where one person hides, and five players seek them

In other news, Forza Horizon 6 has reportedly already sold over 500,000 copies on Steam. Additionally, check out the official FH6 Xbox Wireless Controller and headset. What are your thoughts on Forza Horizon 6’s multiplayer game modes? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced not featuring the Hidden Blade as a ‘selectable weapon’. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Won’t Have Hidden Blade As a ‘Selectable Weapon’

Assassin’s Black Flag Resynced players will not have an option to use the Hidden Blade as a ‘selectable weapon’.

Battlefield 6 Adds New Option To Pre-Order Microtransactions

If you’re desperate and can’t hang on to get your Battlefield 6 microtransactions locked in place, EA has a solution.

Xbox Is Rolling Out Its New Logo To Consoles

In an announcement on Friday, Xbox is going to be rolling out its brand-new logo on all Xbox consoles in several ways.

EXCLUSIVE: Varsity High School Football Will Feature Real Schools

Insider Gaming can exclusively report that Varsity High School Football has reached a deal to include hundreds of real schools in the game.

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1056)—Answers for Saturday, May 2
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to the weekend! It’s the first weekend of May, which came around quickly considering, you know, its proximity to the start of the month… Hopefully, you’ve got something exciting planned, but before you can kickstart the weekend, you’ll want to crush today’s NYT Connections puzzle.

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections hints and answers you need today!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (May 2)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • POST-IT
  • TREASURY
  • HERALDRY
  • PLAY
  • INTERIOR
  • MUSICAL
  • MENTAL
  • STATE
  • TIMES TABLES
  • EDUCATION
  • OPERA
  • PSYCHIC
  • EXTRASENSORY
  • GLOBETROTTER
  • TELEPATHIC
  • BALLET

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Words you’d associate with a clairvoyant
  • Green – Departments of the US Cabinet
  • Blue – Performances you’d watch on a stage
  • Purple – Words that start with names of popular newspapers

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, May 2:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, May 2 (#1056)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – EXTRASENSORY, MENTAL, PSYCHIC, TELEPATHIC
  • Green – EDUCATION, INTERIOR, STATE, TREASURY
  • Blue – BALLET, MUSICAL, OPERA, PLAY
  • Purple – GLOBETROTTER, HERALDRY, POST-IT, TIMES TABLES

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-connections-hints-solutions-may-2-1056/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Battlefield 6 Adds New Option To Pre-Order Microtransactions
item.content:encoded:
The evolution of microtransactions will eventually need to be studied one day, as this is a new one for us. With Battlefield 6 Season 3 on the horizon, you can secure your Battle Pass for the new season and be incentivized to do so.

Battlefield 6 is still retaining a relatively healthy player base nearly seven months on from launch. Don’t get it wrong, though; at least on PC, the game has lost over 90% of its players, but there are still tens of thousands appearing each day, with more on console.

If you are still enjoying Battlefield 6, you might be interested to know about the game’s latest microtransaction-based feature, which almost beggars belief.

Pre-Order The Battlefield 6 Season 3 Battle Pass

If you log into Battlefield 6 right now, there’s a good chance you’ll see the new Battlefield 6 Season 3 advert. Only, it’s nothing to do with upcoming maps or modes; it’s a suggestion for a ‘Next Season Pre-Order.’

As you can see below in Charlie Intel’s post, you can pre-order either the normal Battle Pass or Pro upgrade.

There’s no price reduction for doing so, but you are rewarded for your loyalty with some benefits, just about.

With the normal pass, you get instant access to the Verdant L110 Weapon Package and 2 Tier Skips. Whereas the Pro pass gives you 2 Weapon Packs, the Tier Skips, and an XP boost.

For some players, this will be a handy timesaver to get ready for Season 3, but some may view this for what it is: Pre-paying for extra paid content for the game you already own.

This is one of the more eye-opening things I’ve seen for a while. However, it’s a sign of the times, with companies always looking for the next microtransaction move. Do you agree with this? Let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord.

Read about Xbox rolling out its new logo to consoles and our exclusive story about Varsity High School Football featuring real schools for more news.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/battlefield-6-adds-new-option-to-pre-order-microtransactions/

item.title: Loot Up Codes (May 2026)
item.content:encoded:
Use these Roblox Loot Up monthly redeem codes to get your hands on plenty of free rewards, including Coins, Crystals, and much more.

Loot Up is a popular Roblox experience that drops players into the middle of a fast-paced combat world filled with enemies to defeat and treasures to discover. Players are required to battle foes and collect valuable loot upon launch, and from there, progress through the game to get the best weapons and armor available. With enchantment systems, special magical properties, and challenging bosses on offer, the Roblox game has something for everybody.

Players progress by tackling increasingly difficult areas and boss encounters, which drop rare loot and materials. The game features various weapon types with different attack patterns and special abilities, letting players customize their combat style to their preferences.  

Having been released in early 2025, Loot Up has gradually gained traction over the course of the year and now boasts a fairly dedicated player base. Although simply participating in the various battles the game offers is enough for players to keep upgrading their gear, using redeem codes to get some extra resources is never a bad idea.

Active Loot Up Codes

  • DUNGEON: Great Flame Sword, 500x Coins, 500x Crystals, 5000x EXP, 5 Forgeguard, 5 True Enchant Stones, 2x Ancient Skill Scroll (NEW)
  • Desert: 200x Coins, 200x Crystals, 1000x EXP, 5 Forgeguard, 5 True Enchant Stones, 5 Enchant Stones, 2x Ancient Skill Scroll
  • 10MIL: 100x Coins, 300x Crystals, 1000x EXP, 5 Forgeguard, 5 True Enchant Stones, 5 Enchant Stones, 2x Ancient Skill Scroll
  • PVPUPDATE: 100x Coins, 500x Crystals, 1000x EXP, 5 Forgeguard, 5 True Enchant Stones, 5 Enchant Stones, 2x Ancient Skill Scroll
  • W1UPDATE: 100x Coins, 500x Crystals, 1000x EXP, 5 Forgeguard, 5 True Enchant Stones, 5 Enchant Stones, 2x Ancient Skill Scroll
  • 7MIL: 100x Coins, 500x Crystals, 1000x EXP, 5 Love Stones, 5 Enchant Stones, 2x Ancient Skill Scroll
  • 5MIL: 100x Coins, 500x Crystals, 1000x EXP, 5 Love Stones, 5 Enchant Stones
  • 3MIL: 100x Coins, 500x Crystals, 1000x EXP, 5 Love Stones, 5 Enchant Stones
  • DRAGON: 1000x Coins, 500x Crystals, 1000x EXP, 5 Enchant Stones
  • DELAY: 900x Coins, 500x Crystals, 1000x EXP, 40x Golden Rings
  • AFK: 900x Coins, 500x Crystals, 1000x EXP, 2x True Enchant Stone, 2x Ancient Skill Scroll
  • MORERINGS: 900x Coins, 500x Crystals, 1000x EXP, 40x Golden Rings
  • GOLDENRINGS: 1200x Coins, 1200x Crystals, 1500x EXP, 20x Golden Rings, 3x Skill Scrolls
  • HAPPYNEWYEARS: 1200x Coins, 1200x Crystals, 1500x EXP, 3x Foreguard, 3x Enchant Stones, 20x Frozen Keys, 3x Skill Scrolls
  • CHRISTMAS: 1200x Coins, 1200x Crystals, 1500x EXP, 3x Foreguard, 3x Enchant Stones, 20x Frozen Keys, 3x Skill Scrolls

Expired Loot Up Codes

  • Release

How To Redeem Loot Up Codes

Follow the easy steps listed below to redeem all the active codes in Loot Up:

  1. Boot up Loot Up on Roblox and finish the tutorial
  2. Click on the button labled Social on the left hand side of the screen
  3. Type or paste an active code into the text box
  4. Click on enter to claim the freebie

How To Get More Loot Up Codes

Loot Up codes are typically released through the game’s community and developer channels, but they don’t appear automatically in-game. The best places to catch them early are the official Loot Up Discord server, which has a dedicated codes channel where all new codes are posted first, and the Humbling Games Roblox Group where developers announce updates and milestones.

Codes often come out around game milestones, major updates, and special occasions like holidays, but if you’re only checking sporadically, you’ll very easily miss them because no alert system tells players when a code drops—or when it stops working. Because of this, the easiest strategy is to save this guide in your browser and check back instead of trying to monitor every source yourself.

This page is refreshed as soon as fresh codes go live, and any codes that have expired are taken out quickly so you always see only the ones that still work.

Enjoying our Roblox games codes coverage? Check out the codes for popular games like Steal A Brainrot, Radiant Residents, and Fix It Up on Insider Gaming!

Interested in the latest developments in the world of gaming and esports? Then join the Insider Gaming Discord server and engage with the passionate community.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/roblox-loot-up-codes/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: All Invincible VS Codes & Twitch Drops—How to Redeem Them, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Why only enjoy Invincible VS single-player and multiplayer content? Be rewarded further by claiming free Twitch Drop rewards and redeeming monthly codes to enhance the beauty of beat ’em up.

The Invincible VS series has been brought to life as a proper, hard-hitting 2D fighter. There’s a big audience who’ve been eagerly awaiting it, and you can get your journey started the right way with special cosmetics, courtesy of Twitch Drops and more.

Does Invincible VS Have Monthly Codes?

Invincible VS doesn’t appear to have traditional monthly codes you can redeem in-game for special items and rewards; however, you might have heard about people getting exclusive skins for the game through a certain pizza-based promotion!

This is the definition of: ‘Let me explain.’

So, I haven’t managed to find any codes for Invincible VS, but you will find lots for Invincible Showdown, which is a Roblox game. Therein might lie some confusion when looking up codes.

  • More Insider Gaming: Invincible VS Story Mode Game Length & Difficulties

Currently, there seems to be one place in the world offering Invincible VS codes—Prince Street Pizza.

The American Pizza chain has a fun tie-in with Invincible VS. Order through one of their chains (we’re not sure how much you need to spend), and you’ll be able to claim a code for two exclusive Invincible AKA Mark Grayson skins.

If you’re based outside of the United States, this will REALLY test your love for the franchise.

Here’s a closer look at both skins in action, thanks to @Biscxtti.

All Invincible VS Twitch Drops

You can claim an Invincible VS Title, Background, and Twitch Badge for meeting the Twitch Drop requirements.

These are exclusive rewards and goodies that can only be obtained from Twitch. So if you don’t have an active Twitch account, firstly, you’ll need to register for one—and it’s completely free!

  • More Insider Gaming: 7 New Invincible VS DLC Characters Have Leaked

More on how to redeem later, but the three special rewards are as follows.

How to Get Invincible VS Twitch Drops

You need to watch 30 minutes of applicable, sponsored Invincible VS Twitch footage to claim the Title and Background, whereas you must give a suib or gifted sub to a streamer to claim the Twitch Badge.

How To Get Title and Background Twitch Drops

  1. Go to the main Twitch website.
  2. Make an account, or log into your current one. 
  3. Now, search for Invincible VS.
  4. Watch any streamer playing the game in the Invincible VS category for at least 30 minutes.
  5. Go to the Twitch Drops inventory page.
  6. You should be able to claim a code if you’ve done this correctly.
  7. Go to the Invincible VS website.
  8. Select Login.
  9. Log in to your platform (PlayStation, Xbox, or PC) account.
  10. Select your account name in the upper corner and choose Redeem Code.
  11. Enter your code in the box.
  12. The next time you log into Invincible VS, you should have the Title and Background.

How To Get Twitch Badge

  1. Go to the main Twitch website.
  2. Find the Invincible VS category.
  3. Choose a Streamer in this category.
  4. Click on their profile.
  5. Select Gift A Sub.
  6. If done correctly, you will unlock the Invincible Twitch Badge to equip.

The developer has issued some methods to try if you find Twitch isn’t registering your Invincible VS watched time:

  • ‘Make sure you don’t have the stream muted or minimized. When you mute or minimize streams, Twitch will stop counting you as an active viewer and will pause your watch progress.  
  • Make sure you’re set to appear as “Online” on Twitch.  
  • Log out of Twitch, clear your cache, refresh the page, and log back in.’

Let us know how you get on in your heroic quest to claim Twitch Drops and Codes. Are there any we’ve missed? Message the team via the Insider Gaming Discord, which continues to grow daily.

Finally, check whether Invincible VS has crossplay or not for more guides and help.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/invincible-vs-codes-twitch-drops/
